title: A Branch-and-Price Algorithm for Large-Scale Vehicle Routing with Time Windows
abstract: We develop a novel branch-and-price framework for solving the vehicle routing problem with time windows (VRPTW) on instances with up to 1,000 customers. Our approach combines a labeling algorithm for pricing with tailored branching rules that exploit the structure of feasible routes. Computational experiments on extended Solomon and Gehring–Homberger benchmarks demonstrate that the method closes the optimality gap on 47 previously unsolved instances.

title: Reinforcement Learning for Adaptive Operating Room Scheduling Under Uncertainty
abstract: Operating room scheduling under stochastic surgery durations remains a challenging combinatorial problem. We propose a deep reinforcement learning agent that learns scheduling policies directly from simulated surgical environments. The agent adapts in real time to cancellations and duration overruns, reducing patient wait times by 18% and overtime costs by 23% compared to deterministic baselines on data from a 600-bed teaching hospital.

title: Stochastic Integer Programming for Pandemic Vaccine Distribution Networks
abstract: We formulate a two-stage stochastic integer program for allocating COVID-era vaccine supplies across a hierarchical distribution network. The model captures demand uncertainty, cold-chain logistics constraints, and equity objectives across heterogeneous populations. A sample average approximation scheme with Benders decomposition solves national-scale instances within 2% of optimality in under 30 minutes, yielding distribution plans that improve coverage equity by 31%.

title: Graph Neural Networks for Accelerating Mixed-Integer Programming Solvers
abstract: We train graph neural networks (GNNs) on bipartite variable-constraint representations of mixed-integer programs to predict strong branching decisions. The learned policy is embedded as a callback in SCIP, reducing the number of branch-and-bound nodes by 35% on average across MIPLIB 2024 instances. Transfer experiments show the GNN generalizes from small training instances to significantly larger test problems with minimal performance degradation.

title: A Hybrid Genetic Algorithm with Adaptive Large Neighborhood Search for the Pickup and Delivery Problem
abstract: We present a hybrid metaheuristic that embeds adaptive large neighborhood search (ALNS) operators within a genetic algorithm framework for the pickup and delivery problem with time windows. The population management scheme maintains diversity through a biased fitness function combining cost and structural distance. On standard Ropke–Pisinger benchmarks, the algorithm finds 14 new best-known solutions and matches state-of-the-art results on 89% of remaining instances.

title: Distributionally Robust Nurse Rostering with Preference Learning
abstract: Nurse rostering must balance staffing coverage, regulatory constraints, and individual scheduling preferences. We introduce a distributionally robust optimization model where preference weights are learned from historical swap request data using an inverse optimization approach. The Wasserstein ambiguity set guards against mis-specified preference models. A column generation algorithm solves month-long planning horizons for departments of 120 nurses, yielding rosters that reduce voluntary swap requests by 42%.

title: Electric Vehicle Fleet Routing with Nonlinear Charging and Battery Degradation
abstract: We study the green vehicle routing problem for electric fleets incorporating realistic nonlinear charging curves and long-term battery degradation effects. A piecewise-linear approximation of the charging function is embedded within an adaptive large neighborhood search heuristic. Results on real-world logistics data from a European parcel carrier show that accounting for degradation reduces total cost of ownership by 8% over a five-year horizon while maintaining service level agreements.

title: Online Appointment Scheduling with No-Show Predictions: A Robust Approach
abstract: Patient no-shows cause significant revenue loss and inefficiency in outpatient clinics. We develop a robust online scheduling policy that leverages machine learning predictions of no-show probabilities to overbook appointment slots adaptively. The policy provides worst-case guarantees under a budgeted uncertainty set while exploiting prediction accuracy when forecasts are reliable. A clinical pilot at two ambulatory care centers demonstrates a 15% increase in realized throughput with no increase in patient waiting time.

title: Quantum-Inspired Algorithms for Combinatorial Optimization: A Computational Study
abstract: We benchmark quantum-inspired tensor network algorithms against state-of-the-art classical solvers on a suite of combinatorial optimization problems including Max-Cut, graph coloring, and quadratic assignment. On structured instances up to 5,000 variables, the tensor network approach finds solutions within 1.5% of best known in 10x less wall-clock time than simulated annealing. However, on random unstructured instances, classical methods retain a significant advantage, suggesting that structure exploitation is key to quantum-inspired speedups.

title: Fair Resource Allocation in Emergency Departments: A Multi-Objective Optimization Framework
abstract: Emergency department crowding disproportionately affects vulnerable populations. We propose a multi-objective mixed-integer program that jointly optimizes throughput, equity, and staff workload balance. An epsilon-constraint method generates the Pareto frontier, enabling hospital administrators to navigate trade-offs explicitly. Simulation experiments calibrated on two urban EDs show that Pareto-optimal staffing plans reduce length-of-stay disparities between demographic groups by 27% with less than 3% throughput loss.

title: A Column Generation Approach for Integrated Crew and Vehicle Scheduling in Public Transit
abstract: Crew and vehicle scheduling in public transit are traditionally solved sequentially, leading to suboptimal integrated solutions. We develop a unified Dantzig–Wolfe decomposition where vehicle blocks and crew duties are generated simultaneously via coupled pricing subproblems. The master problem coordinates resources through linking constraints that enforce consistency. On real timetable data from a German transit agency, the integrated approach reduces operational costs by 5.2% compared to the sequential benchmark.

title: Predict-Then-Optimize for Stochastic Inventory Management with Deep Learning Demand Forecasts
abstract: Standard two-stage approaches to data-driven inventory management first fit a demand forecast model and then optimize ordering decisions. We develop an end-to-end predict-then-optimize framework using a surrogate loss function derived from the newsvendor cost structure. A deep learning architecture captures nonlinear demand patterns from high-dimensional feature vectors. Experiments on retail data from a Fortune 500 company show 12% cost reduction versus separate estimation and optimization pipelines.
